prevalence.trend: stable; low detection outside specialist services

burden.mortalityRate: Indirect; elevated self-harm and suicide risk

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11 criteria; requires longitudinal assessment and differentiation from mood disorders

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low due to limited structured psychotherapy access

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Specialist diagnosis in tertiary psychiatry; limited community recognition
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Psychotherapy (DBT/mentalization-based) where available; crisis management and comorbidity treatment
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Low availability of structured psychotherapy and high stigma
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Growing tele-mental health services may improve access to structured psychotherapy in the future
